stem: In triangle LMN, angle M is a right angle, point P lies on segment LN, and point Q lies on segment MN such that segment PQ is parallel to segment LM. If the measure of angle LNM is 52°, what is the measure, in degrees, of angle LPQ?
rationale: Since PQ ∥ LM and ∠M is a right angle, ∠NQP is also a right angle. In triangle NPQ, the angles sum to 180°, so ∠NPQ = 180 − 90 − 52 = 38°. Angle LPQ is supplementary to ∠NPQ (since L, P, N are collinear), so the measure of ∠LPQ is 180 − 38 = 142°.
